channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hope Florida Foundation meeting disrupted by slurs, pornographic images amid scrutiny
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Pornographic images and racial slurs abruptly ended the virtual Hope Florida Foundation meeting Thursday morning. Board members then reconvened later in the afternoon for an hour and 30 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nearly 90% of Consumer Financial Protection Bureau cut as Trump's government downsizing continues
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is drastically shrinking the workforce and mission of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, eviscerating an agency created after the Great Recession with the goal of protecting Americans from fraud, abuse and deceptive practices.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP and the Trump administration argue over presidential access before appeals judges
item.description: The Associated Press and Trump administration were back in a Washington court as part of a high-stakes battle over who can control which journalists can question the president.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Pentagon spokesman tied to online DEI purge was asked to resign, official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Pentagon spokesman John Ullyot was asked to resign this week, a senior defense official told The Associated Press on Thursday, in the latest shakeup for the Defense Department following firings and other changes under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protesters gather outside El Salvador’s consulate in St. Paul
item.description: Protesters gathered outside El Salvador’s consulate in St. Paul on Wednesday to demand the return of a man who was mistakenly sent to a prison in that Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump officials' defiance over Abrego Garcia's deportation is 'shocking,' appeals court says
item.description: A federal appeals court says it is “shocking” that The Trump administration claims it can’t do anything to free Kilmar Abrego Garcia from an El Salvador prison and return him to the U.S. A three-judge panel from the 4th U.S.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New bill could allow for sale of wine at New York grocery stores
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (WIVB) — A new bill recently introduced in the New York State Senate could soon allow for the sale of wine in grocery stores across New York State.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Trump says trade deals could be done within a month
item.description: After saying earlier in the day that he was “in no rush” to finish trade deals, President Donald Trump said he thought he could wrap up tariff talks “over the next three or four weeks.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Marshall man claims ICE detained him for protests over police killings
item.description: A Marshall man was detained by federal immigration authorities who claim he overstayed his student visa after it was revoked. However, he tells MPR News from the Kandiyohi County Jail that his arrest may have been politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis council member Emily Koski drops out of mayor's race
item.description: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski has dropped out of the mayor’s race, according to a campaign update Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Will the SAVE Act make it harder for married women to vote? We ask legal experts
item.description: Legal experts say states could help married women who have changed their last names by accepting documents like a legal decree or a marriage certificate, but it might not fix the issue for all.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration says it will exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from ‘reciprocal’ tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Bondi appoints new interim U.S. Attorney for San Diego, Imperial counties
item.description: The Southern District of California will have a new U.S attorney after Attorney General Pam Bondi appointed a longtime San Diego prosecutor to take over the role as the region's top federal law enforcement official following Tara McGrath's termination.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
